Check that the PCB works correctly.

source_refs.source_quote: F.27 Safety shutdown: Flame simulation

possible_causes: The gas pressure monitor has been triggered
The thermal isolator device has been triggered
manufacturer_steps: Check that the ignition transformer works correctly.
Using an installation with a flue non-return flap, check whether the diagnostics code D.085 has been set correctly. (→ Page 19)
Check whether the condensate discharge is connected correctly. (→ Page 18)
If the drain is blocked, check whether the heat exchanger's internal insulating material is OK.

Check that the fan works correctly.

possible_causes: D.149 = 6, no feedback from the pump
D.149 = 8, flow rate at the end of the purge programme is insufficient
manufacturer_steps: Call up diagnostics code D.149 to obtain further information about the malfunction.
Remove the blockage from the pump.
Check the pressure of the hydraulic circuit; ensure that there is no air in the circuit.
Check that the plugs are connected correctly.
The detected pump is not correct for the product code; check the product code.
Use the pump that has the correct article number.
Check whether the stopcocks and thermostatic valves are open.
Check the filling pressure; purge the circuit.

If fault F.75 occurs, read the explanation below for the relevant value of the diagnostics code in order to analyse the problem. 0 = No fault, 1 = Pump blocked, 2 = Electrical pump fault, 3 = Pump dry run, 5 = Pressure sensor fault, 6 = No feedback from the pump, 7 = Incorrect pump detected, 8 = Flow rate at the end of the purge programme is insufficient

safety_warnings.text: Imminent danger to life or risk of severe personal injury

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Imminent danger to life or risk of severe personal injury

safety_warnings.text: Risk of death from electric shock

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Risk of death from electric shock

safety_warnings.text: Risk of minor personal injury

source_refs.source_quote: Warning. Risk of minor personal injury

safety_warnings.text: Risk of material or environmental damage

source_refs.source_quote: Caution. Risk of material or environmental damage

safety_warnings.text: The following work must only be carried out by competent persons who are sufficiently qualified to do so: Set-up, Dismantling, Installation, Start-up, Inspection and maintenance, Repair, Decommissioning. Proceed in accordance with current technology.

source_refs.source_quote: The following work must only be carried out by competent persons who are sufficiently qualified to do so: Set-up Dismantling Installation Start-up Inspection and maintenance Repair Decommissioning ► Proceed in accordance with current tech- nology.

safety_warnings.text: What to do if you smell gas in the building: Avoid rooms that smell of gas. If possible, open doors and windows fully and ensure adequate ventilation. Do not use naked flames (e.g. lighters, matches). Do not smoke. Do not use any electrical switches, mains plugs, doorbells, telephones or other communication systems in the building. Close the emergency control valve or the main isolator. If possible, close the gas isolator cock on the product.

source_refs.source_quote: What to do if you smell gas in the building: ► Avoid rooms that smell of gas. ► If possible, open doors and windows fully and ensure adequate ventilation. ► Do not use naked flames (e.g. lighters, matches). ► Do not smoke. ► Do not use any electrical switches, mains plugs, doorbells, telephones or other com- munication systems in the building. ► Close the emergency control valve or the main isolator. ► If possible, close the gas isolator cock on the product.

safety_warnings.text: What to do if you smell gas in the building: Avoid rooms that smell of gas. If possible, open doors and windows fully and ensure adequate ventilation. Do not use naked flames (e.g. lighters, matches). Do not smoke. Do not use any electrical switches, mains plugs, doorbells, telephones or other communication systems in the building. If it is safe to do so, close the emergency control valve or the main isolator. If possible, close the gas stopcock on the product. Warn other occupants in the building by yelling or banging on doors or walls. Leave the building immediately and ensure that others do not enter the building. Notify the gas supply company or the National Grid +44 (0) 800 111999 by telephone once you are outside of the building.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.3 Risk of death from escaping gas
source_refs.source_quote: What to do if you smell gas in the building: ► Avoid rooms that smell of gas. If possible, open doors and windows fully and ensure adequate ventilation. ► Do not use naked flames (e.g. lighters, matches). ► Do not smoke. ► Do not use any electrical switches, mains plugs, doorbells, telephones or other com- munication systems in the building. ► If it is safe to do so, close the emergency control valve or the main isolator. If possible, close the gas stopcock on the product. ► Warn other occupants in the building by yelling or banging on doors or walls. ► Leave the building immediately and ensure that others do not enter the building. ► Notify the gas supply company or the Na- tional Grid +44 (0) 800 111999 by tele- phone once you are outside of the build- ing.

safety_warnings.topic: Leaks if product installed below ground level (Liquid gas)
safety_warnings.text: Liquid gas accumulates at floor level. If the product is installed below ground level, liquid gas may accumulate at floor level if there are any leaks. In this case, there is a risk of explosion. Make sure that liquid gas cannot escape from the product or the gas pipe under any circumstances.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.4 Risk of death from leaks if the product is installed below ground level
source_refs.source_quote: Liquid gas accumulates at floor level. If the product is installed below ground level, liquid gas may accumulate at floor level if there are any leaks. In this case, there is a risk of explosion. ► Make sure that liquid gas cannot escape from the product or the gas pipe under any circumstances.

safety_warnings.topic: Blocked or leaking flue gas routes
safety_warnings.text: Installation errors, damage, tampering, impermissible installation sites or similar can cause flue gas to escape and result in a risk of poisoning. What to do if you smell flue gas in the property: Open all accessible doors and windows fully to provide ventilation. Switch off the product. Check the flue gas routes in the product and the flue gas diversions.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.5 Risk of death due to blocked or leaking flue gas routes
source_refs.source_quote: Installation errors, damage, tampering, imper- missible installation sites or similar can cause flue gas to escape and result in a risk of pois- oning. What to do if you smell flue gas in the prop- erty: ► Open all accessible doors and windows fully to provide ventilation. ► Switch off the product. ► Check the flue gas routes in the product and the flue gas diversions.

safety_warnings.text: Do not use the product in storage rooms that contain explosive or flammable substances (such as petrol, paper or paint).

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.6 Risk of death due to explosive and flammable materials
source_refs.source_quote: ► Do not use the product in storage rooms that contain explosive or flammable sub- stances (such as petrol, paper or paint).

safety_warnings.text: The basic diagrams included in this document do not show all safety devices required for correct installation. Install the necessary safety devices in the installation. Observe the applicable national and international laws, standards and directives.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.7 Risk of death due to lack of safety devices
source_refs.source_quote: The basic diagrams included in this docu- ment do not show all safety devices required for correct installation. ► Install the necessary safety devices in the installation. ► Observe the applicable national and inter- national laws, standards and directives.

safety_warnings.text: There is a risk of death from electric shock if you touch live components. Before commencing work on the product: Unplug the mains plug. Or disconnect the product from the power supply by switching off all power supplies (electrical partition with a contact gap of at least 3 mm, e.g. fuse or circuit breaker). Secure against being switched back on again. Wait for at least 3 minutes until the capacitors have discharged. Check that there is no voltage.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.8 Risk of death from electric shock
source_refs.source_quote: There is a risk of death from electric shock if you touch live components. Before commencing work on the product: ► Unplug the mains plug. ► Or disconnect the product from the power supply by switching off all power supplies (electrical partition with a contact gap of at least 3 mm, e.g. fuse or circuit breaker). ► Secure against being switched back on again. ► Wait for at least 3 minutes until the capa- citors have discharged. ► Check that there is no voltage.

safety_warnings.topic: Escaping flue gas (Not for Great Britain)
safety_warnings.text: If you operate the product with an empty condensate siphon, flue gas may escape into the room air. In order to operate the product, ensure that the condensate siphon is always full. Condition: Permitted B23 or B23P unit types with condensate siphon (third-party accessory) Water seal level: ≥ 200 mm

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.9 Risk of death from escaping flue gas
source_refs.source_quote: If you operate the product with an empty con- densate siphon, flue gas may escape into the room air. ► In order to operate the product, ensure that the condensate siphon is always full. Condition: Permitted B23 or B23P unit types with condensate siphon (third-party accessory) Water seal level: ≥ 200 mm

safety_warnings.text: If you operate the product with an empty condensate trap / siphon, then flue gas may escape into the room air. In order to operate the product, ensure that the condensate trap / siphon is always full. Condition: Permitted B23 or B23P unit types with condensate siphon (third-party accessory) Water seal level: ≥ 200 mm

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.10 Risk of death from escaping flue gas
source_refs.source_quote: If you operate the product with an empty con- densate trap / siphon, then flue gas may es- cape into the room air. ► In order to operate the product, ensure that the condensate trap / siphon is always full. Condition: Permitted B23 or B23P unit types with condensate siphon (third-party accessory) Water seal level: ≥ 200 mm

safety_warnings.text: Cabinet-type casing can give rise to dangerous situations when used on a product which is operated with an open flue. Ensure that the product is supplied with sufficient combustion air.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.11 Risk of death due to cabinet-type casing
source_refs.source_quote: Cabinet-type casing can give rise to danger- ous situations when used on a product which is operated with an open flue. ► Ensure that the product is supplied with sufficient combustion air.

safety_warnings.text: Condition: Open-flued operation. Ensure that the air supply to the product's installation room is permanently unobstructed and sufficient in accordance with the relevant ventilation requirements.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.12 Risk of poisoning caused by insufficient combustion air supply
source_refs.source_quote: Condition: Open-flued operation ► Ensure that the air supply to the product's installation room is permanently unobstruc- ted and sufficient in accordance with the relevant ventilation requirements.

safety_warnings.text: Only operate the product if the air/flue pipe has been completely installed. With the exception of short periods for testing purposes, only operate the product when the front casing is installed and closed.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.13 Risk of poisoning and burns caused by escaping hot flue gases
source_refs.source_quote: ► Only operate the product if the air/flue pipe has been completely installed. ► With the exception of short periods for testing purposes, only operate the product when the front casing is installed and closed.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of being burned or scalded by hot components. Only carry out work on these components once they have cooled down.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.14 Risk of being burned or scalded by hot components
source_refs.source_quote: ► Only carry out work on these components once they have cooled down.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of injury due to the heavy weight of the product. Make sure that the product is transported by at least two people.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.15 Risk of injury due to the heavy weight of the product
source_refs.source_quote: ► Make sure that the product is transported by at least two people.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of material damage caused by using an unsuitable tool. Use the correct tool.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.16 Risk of material damage caused by using an unsuitable tool
source_refs.source_quote: ► Use the correct tool.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of material damage caused by frost. Do not install the product in rooms prone to frost.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.17 Risk of material damage caused by frost
source_refs.source_quote: ► Do not install the product in rooms prone to frost.

safety_warnings.topic: Corrosion damage due to unsuitable combustion and room air
safety_warnings.text: Sprays, solvents, chlorinated cleaning agents, paint, adhesives, ammonia compounds, dust or similar substances may lead to corrosion on the product and in the flue system. Ensure that the supply of combustion air is always free of fluorine, chlorine, sulphur, dust, etc. Ensure that no chemical substances are stored at the installation site. If you are installing the product in hairdressing salons, painter's or joiner's workshops, cleaning businesses or similar locations, choose a separate installation room in which the room air is technically free of chemical substances.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.18 Risk of corrosion damage due to unsuitable combustion and room air
source_refs.source_quote: Sprays, solvents, chlorinated cleaning agents, paint, adhesives, ammonia com- pounds, dust or similar substances may lead to corrosion on the product and in the flue system. ► Ensure that the supply of combustion air is always free of fluorine, chlorine, sulphur, dust, etc. ► Ensure that no chemical substances are stored at the installation site. ► If you are installing the product in hairdressing salons, painter's or joiner's workshops, cleaning businesses or similar locations, choose a separate installation room in which the room air is technically free of chemical substances.

safety_warnings.topic: Corrosion damage due to unsuitable combustion and room air (Except Great Britain)
safety_warnings.text: Ensure that the combustion air is not routed through chimneys which have previously been used with floor-standing oil-fired boilers, or with other boilers, which could cause soot to build up in the chimney.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.18 Risk of corrosion damage due to unsuitable combustion and room air
source_refs.source_quote: Applicability: Except Great Britain ► Ensure that the combustion air is not routed through chimneys which have previously been used with floor-standing oil-fired boilers, or with other boilers, which could cause soot to build up in the chimney.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of material damage caused by leak detection sprays and fluids. Leak detection sprays and fluids block the filter for the mass flow sensor on the Venturi, thereby destroying the mass flow sensor. During repair work, do not apply any leak detection sprays or fluids to the covering cap on the filter for the Venturi.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.19 Risk of material damage caused by leak detection sprays and fluids
source_refs.source_quote: Leak detection sprays and fluids block the filter for the mass flow sensor on the Venturi, thereby destroying the mass flow sensor. ► During repair work, do not apply any leak detection sprays or fluids to the covering cap on the filter for the Venturi.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of damage to the flexible gas pipe. The corrugated gas pipe may become damaged if weight is placed on it. Do not suspend the compact thermal module on the flexible gas pipe, for example during maintenance work.

source_refs.section_title: 1.3.20 Risk of damage to the flexible gas pipe
source_refs.source_quote: The corrugated gas pipe may become dam- aged if weight is placed on it. ► Do not suspend the compact thermal module on the flexible gas pipe, for example during maintenance work.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of material damage caused by incorrect handling. Incorrect settings at installer level may cause damage and operating faults to the heating installation. You must only access the installer level if you are an approved competent person.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution. Risk of material damage caused by incor- rect handling. Incorrect settings at installer level may cause damage and operating faults to the heating installation. ► You must only access the installer level if you are an approved competent person.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of material damage due to poor-quality heating water. Ensure that the heating water is of sufficient quality.

source_refs.section_title: Checking and treating the heating water/filling and supplementary water
source_refs.source_quote: Caution. Risk of material damage due to poor-qual- ity heating water ► Ensure that the heating water is of suffi- cient quality.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of material damage if the heating water is treated with unsuitable additives. Unsuitable additives may cause changes in the components, noises in heating mode and possibly subsequent damage. Do not use any unsuitable antifreeze and corrosion inhibitors, biocides or sealants.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution. Risk of material damage if the heating water is treated with unsuitable additives. Unsuitable additives may cause changes in the components, noises in heating mode and possibly subsequent damage. ► Do not use any unsuitable antifreeze and corrosion inhibitors, biocides or sealants.

safety_warnings.text: An incorrect gas type setting may cause malfunctions or a reduction in the service life of the product. If the product design does not match the local gas type, this may lead to malfunctions or to some components wearing prematurely. Before you start up the product, compare the gas type information on the data plate with the gas type group available at the installation site.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution. An incorrect gas type setting may cause malfunctions or a reduction in the service life of the product. If the product design does not match the local gas type, this may lead to malfunctions or to some components wearing prematurely. ► Before you start up the product, compare the gas type information on the data plate with the gas type group available at the installation site.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of material damage and operating faults caused by incorrect gas flow pressure. If the gas flow pressure lies outside the permissible range, this can cause operating faults in and damage to the product. Do not make any adjustments to the product. Check the gas installation. Do not start up the product.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution. Risk of material damage and operating faults caused by incorrect gas flow pres- sure. If the gas flow pressure lies outside the per- missible range, this can cause operating faults in and damage to the product. ► Do not make any adjustments to the product. ► Check the gas installation. Do not start up the product.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of death from legionella. Legionella multiply at temperatures below 60 °C. Ensure that the end user is familiar with all of the Anti-legionella measures in order to comply with the applicable regulations regarding legionella prevention.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Risk of death from legionella. Legionella multiply at temperatures below 60 °C. ► Ensure that the end user is familiar with all of the Anti-legionella measures in or- der to comply with the applicable regula- tions regarding legionella prevention.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of death and risk of material damage caused by hot flue gas. The seal, heat insulation and self-locking nuts on the burner flange must not be damaged. Otherwise, hot flue gases may escape and cause personal injury and material damage. Replace the seal each time you open the burner flange. Replace the self-locking nuts on the burner flange each time you open the burner flange. If the heat insulation on the burner flange or on the back panel of the heat ex- changer shows signs of damage, replace the heat insulation.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger! Risk of death and risk of material damage caused by hot flue gas. The seal, heat insulation and self-locking nuts on the burner flange must not be damaged. Otherwise, hot flue gases may escape and cause personal injury and material damage. ► Replace the seal each time you open the burner flange. Replace the self-locking nuts on the burner flange each time you open the burner flange. ► If the heat insulation on the burner flange or on the back panel of the heat ex- changer shows signs of damage, replace the heat insulation.

safety_warnings.text: Risk of poisoning due to escaping flue gas. Mineral-oil-based greases can damage the seals.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution. Risk of poisoning due to escaping flue gas. Mineral-oil-based greases can damage the seals.
